diagrams.title: 3D Coordinate System with Transformations
diagrams.description: Shows a 3D coordinate system with X (red), Y (green), and Z (blue) axes originating from center. A unit cube is displayed at the origin, with transformed copies showing translation along axes, rotation around Y-axis, and uniform scaling. This visual introduction helps students understand how 3D objects are positioned and transformed in space.
elements: 3D coordinate axes with arrows and labels (X, Y, Z)
Unit cube at origin in wireframe or solid style
Rotated cube showing 45-degree rotation around Y-axis
Color legend identifying each transformation type
diagrams.purpose: Introduces the fundamental 3D coordinate system and demonstrates the three basic geometric transformations (translation, rotation, scaling) on a visual 3D object

diagrams.title: 3D Rotation: Axis Rotation Visualization
diagrams.description: Three separate views showing a 3D asymmetric object (an L-shaped prism) rotating individually around each major axis. Each panel displays the rotation angle and direction using curved arrows. The rotation is shown from 0 to 90 degrees in discrete steps, illustrating how rotation matrices transform points in 3D space.
elements: Three panel layout (X, Y, Z rotation)
Rotation axis highlighted as a vertical line through object
Rotation matrix equation displayed below each panel
diagrams.purpose: Explains how 3D rotation works around each axis independently, demonstrating the mathematical rotation matrices (Rx, Ry, Rz) and helping students visualize the effect of each rotation

diagrams.title: Homogeneous Transformation Matrix
diagrams.description: A detailed 4x4 matrix diagram showing the structure of the homogeneous transformation matrix used in 3D computer graphics. The matrix is color-coded into four regions: rotation/scaling (3x3 top-left), translation (3x1 top-right), perspective row (1x3 bottom-left), and homogeneous scaling (1x1 bottom-right). Sample values demonstrate a composite transformation.
elements: Vector-matrix multiplication diagram showing point transformation
diagrams.purpose: Explains the mathematical structure of the 4x4 homogeneous transformation matrix, showing how rotation, scaling, and translation are combined into a single matrix for efficient 3D transformations in computer graphics
